programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: I Was Born in Japan and Called Gaijin Chuk Besher, who was born and raised in Japan, talks about being called a gaijin or an outsider, which prompts him to ask questions about identity and diversity.
programme.title: Chatroom Japan

programme.desc: Cosmetic Sponges Experts talk about the evolution of cosmetic sponges and how they are manufactured to achieve their specific capabilities.
programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions

programme.desc: Japanophiles: Johan Nordstrom Johan Nordstrom, a film scholar from Sweden, discusses century-old movies and Japan's unique tradition of live narration for silent films.
programme.title: Japanology Plus

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Bon Odori: Rhythm and Connection The filmmakers explore how the Bon Odori dance serves as a global medium that brings together individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds.
programme.title: NEO-JAPONISM

programme.desc: S01 E02 — Kishu The filmmakers shed light on the eco-friendly charcoal production in Kishu, Japan, highlighting the process of cutting down suitable trees while promoting forest regrowth.
programme.title: A Culture Built with Wood

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Yakumi: Traditional Japanese Seasoning The hosts learn about Japan's food culture as they explore the country to find unique Japanese food and attempt to trace their original sources.
programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Putting Himself Into Every Grain: Seki Tomoharu, Rice Farmer Seki Tomoharu, a farmer, sheds light on his legendary rice that has won international awards and discusses green farming amid rice shortages due to global warming.
programme.title: The Professionals

programme.desc: Tonkotsu Ramen: Fukuoka The filmmakers explore the birthplace of tonkotsu ramen in Fukuoka prefecture to learn about the differences between Hakata and Nagahama styles.
programme.title: Ramen Japan

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: A New Look at Shadows The hosts explore innovative ways artists use shadows in art and design to change how people see the world.
programme.title: Design X Stories

programme.desc: The Search for Puckoo's Scales Rockie and a range of other puppets, including a snail, hippopotamus and crocodile, go on adventures and learn about school life, growing up and fairness.
programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends

programme.desc: Convert to Heat The host explores technologies people rely on daily and breaks down the engineering secrets hidden inside.
programme.title: Take Tech

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Experts help uncover the history of Japan and its people through important stories vital to the country's culture.
programme.title: History Uncovered

programme.desc: Mt. Fuji Long Trail / Beauty Kyle Card hikes the designated long trail around majestic Mt Fuji and discovers its secrets.
programme.title: Cinematic Journey

programme.desc: A Summer Bedtime Routine The filmmakers explore Edo townspeople's ways of repelling mosquitoes during hot and humid summer nights.
programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Lava Flows The hazard map for Mount Fuji, which was revised in 2021, reveals the possibility of greater damage from lava flows. Experts look at what lies behind those assumptions and what countermeasures can be taken.
programme.title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life

programme.desc: Designs that Connect Gaps Graphic designer Sato Taku visits Miyazaki to explore the crowded signs of the city's local bars for inspiration.
programme.title: Design Museum Japan

programme.desc: The Revenge of the Soga Brothers Exploring the world of Kabuki, featuring modern themes and Kabuki's creative staging.
programme.title: Kabuki Kool

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Bon Odori: Rhythm and Connection The filmmakers explore how the Bon Odori dance serves as a global medium that brings together individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds.

An ex-convict bandit (Marlon Brando) finds the partner (Karl Malden) who betrayed him is now a town sheriff with a wife (Katy Jurado) and daughter.
programme.title: One-Eyed Jacks

programme.desc: Capt. Jones (Anthony Hopkins), William Brewster (Richard Crenna), Priscilla Mullins (Jenny Agutter) and company have a rough trip on the Mayflower.
programme.title: Mayflower: The Pilgrims' Adventure

programme.desc: The muscular hero forms Ursa Major and fights space robots to save a princess who has been kidnapped.
programme.title: Hercules

programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm (Dean Martin) and a blonde (Sharon Tate) track $1 billion in gold in the last of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Wrecking Crew

programme.desc: Blue-collar buddies (Sidney Poitier, Bill Cosby) search the underworld for a winning lottery ticket lost in a nightclub holdup.
programme.title: Uptown Saturday Night

programme.desc: An American secret agent tries to retrieve a roll of microfilm in South Africa before it falls into communist hands.
programme.title: The Cape Town Affair

programme.desc: After being fired from his office job, Harold Diddlebock (Harold Lloyd) gets drunk, goes on a wild binge, and buys a near-bankrupt circus.
programme.title: The Sin of Harold Diddlebock

programme.desc: A Supreme Court justice on a duck-hunting foray becomes involved in serious trouble with the citizens in a small town.
programme.title: A Stranger in Town

programme.desc: Kipling's boy hero Mowgli (Sabu), lost in the jungle, is adopted by animals and taught how they talk.
programme.title: Jungle Book

programme.desc: A newspaper editor (Jeffrey Lynn) blasts big business, then needs a big-business man's (Donald Crisp) help.
programme.title: Home Town Story

programme.desc: A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.
programme.title: Zorba the Greek

programme.desc: Blondie and Dagwood Bumstead (Penny Singleton, Arthur Lake) lose Baby Dumpling (Larry Simms) as well as Daisy the dog.
programme.title: Blondie Brings Up Baby

programme.desc: Veterans of the 82nd Airborne Division devise an elaborate plot to simultaneously rob five casinos on New Year's Eve.
programme.title: Ocean's Eleven

programme.desc: A widowed lawyer with two bright children defends a black man falsely accused of raping a white woman in the 1930s Alabama.
programme.title: To Kill a Mockingbird

programme.desc: Buffalo Bill (Roy Rogers) and the cavalry protect mining surveyors from Indians in New Mexico.
programme.title: Young Buffalo Bill

programme.desc: A rancher's (John Wayne) wife (Maureen O'Hara) summons him home to hunt down the outlaw (Richard Boone) and gang who kidnapped his grandson.
programme.title: Big Jake

programme.desc: A novitiate leaves her convent and becomes governess to Capt. Von Trapp's seven children in Austria before World War II.
programme.title: The Sound of Music

programme.desc: Slapstick comedy from Larry, Moe, Curly and occasionally Shemp.
programme.title: The Three Stooges

programme.desc: S06 E06 — Lucy Gets Jack Benny's Account Lucy designs a vault in an attempt to get Jack Benny's account.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: Adm. Kirk (William Shatner), Mr. Spock (Leonard Nimoy), Dr. McCoy (DeForest Kelley) and crew embark in the overhauled Enterprise to find an intelligent entity called VGER.
programme.title: Star Trek: The Motion Picture

programme.desc: A woman (Lizabeth Scott) kills her husband and plots with a private eye (Dan Duryea) after someone tosses a moneybag into her car.
programme.title: Too Late for Tears

programme.desc: Socialite Irene Bullock needs a forgotten man to win a scavenger hunt and finds Godfrey Park, who resides in a dump by the East River. She hires him as a butler, to the chagrin of her spoiled sister Cornelia, who seeks to get Godfrey fired.
programme.title: My Man Godfrey

programme.desc: A brother (Fred Astaire) and sister (Jane Powell) take their act to 1947 London. Includes Astaire's famous dance on the ceiling.
programme.title: Royal Wedding

programme.desc: Dr. Stein (Peter Cushing) and his assistant (Francis Matthews) sew up a new monster, Karl (Michael Gwynn).
programme.title: The Revenge of Frankenstein

programme.desc: A Maine lobsterwoman (Doris Day) has her lawyer (Jack Lemmon) sue a railroad tycoon (Ernie Kovacs) over a spoiled shipment.
programme.title: It Happened to Jane

programme.desc: The outlaw (Wendell Corey) tries to kill an insurance agent (Bob Hope) who has been mistaken for him in order to collect on a big policy.
programme.title: Alias Jesse James

programme.desc: An English girl's dream of racing her horse in the Grand National thunders to reality with the help of a former jockey.
programme.title: National Velvet

programme.desc: Incompatible friends (Bob Hope, Lucille Ball), taken for granted by their spouses, fall in love after having to spend time alone together.
programme.title: The Facts of Life

programme.desc: A fashion photographer (Fred Astaire) turns a Greenwich Village bookworm (Audrey Hepburn) into a Paris cover girl.
programme.title: Funny Face

programme.desc: Doc Holliday (Kirk Douglas) joins Wyatt Earp (Burt Lancaster) and his brothers for a Tombstone showdown with the Clanton gang.
programme.title: Gunfight at the O.K. Corral

programme.desc: Two buddies win a car and head for Hollywood to meet Anita Ekberg.
programme.title: Hollywood or Bust

programme.desc: An Army deserter (Ray Milland) joins a gambler (Barbara Stanwyck) and a miner (Barry Fitzgerald) against a former slave-trader building a private empire.
programme.title: California

programme.desc: A declared-dead man (Brian Donlevy) hides out with a widow (Ella Raines) after his wife and her lover botch his murder.
programme.title: Impact

programme.desc: Private eye Mike Hammer (Mickey Spillane) asks a bikinied blonde (Shirley Eaton) and others about his long-lost secretary, Velda.
programme.title: The Girl Hunters

programme.desc: Billy the Kid (Jack Buetel), Doc Holliday (Walter Huston) and Pat Garrett hang out with a hussy (Jane Russell).
programme.title: The Outlaw

programme.desc: Bumpkins Moe, Larry and Curly Joe (The Three Stooges) help an ice-skating Snow White (Carol Heiss) find her Prince Charming (Edson Stroll).
programme.title: Snow White and the Three Stooges

programme.desc: Married lawyers (Spencer Tracy, Katharine Hepburn) clash in and out of court over a woman's (Judy Holliday) right to shoot her husband and his lover.
programme.title: Adam's Rib

programme.desc: New York playgirl Holly Golightly puzzles a writer who lives in her building.
programme.title: Breakfast at Tiffany's

programme.desc: Tragedy follows when a new-to-town, troublemaking teen meets other anguished children of feckless parents.
programme.title: Rebel Without a Cause

programme.desc: Clumsy Inspector Jacques Clouseau (Peter Sellers) chases a jewel thief (David Niven) and his nephew (Robert Wagner).
programme.title: The Pink Panther

programme.desc: Friends (Guy Madison, Kim Novak, Brian Keith) join a college student's scheme to rob a Reno casino for kicks.
programme.title: 5 Against the House

programme.desc: A singing comedian (Dean Martin) flops on his own, then soars with a sidekick (Jerry Lewis) but insists on hogging the show.
programme.title: The Stooge

programme.desc: Bungling Inspector Clouseau (Peter Sellers) once again seeks the Pink Panther diamond and maddens his boss (Herbert Lom).
programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther

programme.desc: A gruff neighbor (Lee Marvin) and a newspaper editor (Gary Merrill) help a teenage farmer (Brandon de Wilde) grow up in circa-1910 Missouri.
programme.title: The Missouri Traveler

programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm (Dean Martin) and a blonde (Sharon Tate) track $1 billion in gold in the last of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Wrecking Crew

J Docs Presents 263 Nights: The Miracle of Survival The true story of the only victims of the Minsk Ghetto, twenty-six individuals who managed to survive 263 days and nights in a basement during World War II.
programme.title: J Docs Presents

programme.desc: J Docs Presents Inked: Our Stories Remarked How do we preserve memory? Inked explores a powerful new phenomenon: third-generation descendants of Holocaust survivors choosing to honor their ancestors through commemorative tattoos as the generation who lived it fades from view.
programme.title: J Docs Presents

programme.desc: J Docs Presents Sevap Three historic enemies join forces to save the Dead Sea from disappearing.
programme.title: J Docs Presents

programme.desc: J Docs Presents See You Soon An animated short film inspired by true events, Ruwen's letter is a family memory and document that represents the healing of a collective wound and a thread of hope between two siblings separated by a historic event that marked humanity, the Shoah.
programme.title: J Docs Presents

programme.desc: Escape From Treblinka: The Joseph Polonski Story
programme.title: Escape From Treblinka: The Joseph Polonski Story

programme.desc: S01 E108 — Dukla, Poland Joining Polish historian, genealogist and guide, Dr. Tomasz Cebulski, on an aerial and walking tour of Dukla, Poland, a shtetl in Southeast Poland whose Jewish community was decimated by the Nazis during World War II.
programme.title: Aerial & Walking Tours of Jewish Poland
